title: Golden Eggs
excerpt: The boiled egg is a lifesaver for many of us when we don't know what to make. Some of us remember the smell of hard-boiled eggs as part of padkos. Now let's look at how to make the perfect boiled egg. Bring water in a pot to …
html:
The boiled egg is a lifesaver for many of us when we don’t know what to make. Some of us remember the smell of hard-boiled eggs as part of padkos. Now let’s look at how to make the perfect boiled egg.

Bring water in a pot to the boil. Now:

* for a soft yellow and white – boil for 3-4 minutes

* soft yellow and slightly firmer white – boil for 7-8 minutes

*hard-boiled egg – boil for 11-12 minutes

Once your egg is cooked, pour off the hot water and run cold water over it. Let it stand for a bit or you'll burn your hands. Now roll the egg on the table. The shell will crack and you'll be able to peel the egg easily.

The versatile boiled egg can be mashed and mixed with good mayonnaise, salt and pepper. Lovely on a sandwich.

Making the perfect scrambled egg isn’t difficult. Take 2 eggs per person. Whisk the eggs in a cup with a fork. Add 10 ml tap water and whisk further.

Heat your pan on a plate and put 15 ml (3 teaspoons) oil in your pan. Once it's nice and hot, pour in your whisked egg. Let it set for a bit. Add salt and pepper. Now stir so it breaks into pieces with a fork. The egg mustn't be dry.

  • For a special scrambled egg you can sauté onions in a pan and then add the egg.
  • Tomato and mixed herbs can be added.
  • Cheese of your choice can be stirred in.
  • Fry bacon and stir it into the egg.

Eat your scrambled egg on a slice of toast and you'll have a breakfast that will keep you going until lunch.

Sometimes we're afraid to make an omelette . An omelette is really just an extension of the scrambled egg.

Use 3 eggs per person. Whisk the eggs well. If you want a fluffy omelette, you can whisk the egg whites separately until they form peaks. Then fold them into the whisked yolks. Otherwise just whisk the 3 eggs together with 15 ml milk.

Make sure your little pan has 15ml oil and 5 ml butter in it. Let it heat up.

Pour your egg mixture into the pan. Don't touch it at all at first. As it starts to set, push the edges in so the runny egg fills the space. Once the egg is almost completely set, add your filling. Fold the omelette over like a sandwich. Slide it out onto your plate.

Your filling is your choice:

  • just cheese
  • bacon and cheese
  • fried mushrooms
  • salmon and cream cheese
  • herbs of your choice
  • fried tomato and onion
  • spinach and feta
  • lightly fried ham

Making the perfect baked egg is within everyone's reach

Don't make the pan too hot.

Use 10ml oil and 20 ml butter together in your pan.

If you want to cook the egg faster without turning it, put a lid on the pan.

Fancy eggs

Grease the cups of a muffin pan.

Take a slice of bread and flatten it with a rolling pin or a wine bottle. Place it in the muffin pan.

Whisk an egg in a cup. Add chopped tomato, 3 tablespoons cheese, 3 tablespoons chopped ham and salt and pepper to the egg. Pour it over the bread in the muffin pan.

Bake at 180°C for 20 minutes. Take it out of your pan and sprinkle more cheese and chopped spring onions over it. Enjoy it.
